title: Source to Shore filed-memory payoff
summary: Add a compact filed-state payoff for the completed three-beat Source to Shore chapter across existing station, atlas, journal, and notebook surfaces.

execution_order.goal: Scope the smallest content payoff that makes filed Source to Shore feel remembered without becoming a new system.
execution_order.why_now: Once the beta chapter has a container, it needs a quiet payoff that rewards the completed arc.

execution_order.goal: Implement the compact filed-memory payoff using existing station, atlas, journal, notebook, or content-note seams.
execution_order.why_now: A remembered chapter makes the route feel game-like while staying lighter than a new beat.

execution_order.goal: Review the payoff for science accuracy, copy budget, surface fit, and no fourth-beat implication.
execution_order.why_now: Chapter payoff copy should feel satisfying without creating false progression promises.

completion_gate: Filed Source to Shore has one compact payoff that reinforces the high-source to forest-release to coastal-catch relationship.
All changed facts and ecological claims remain science-safe and ledger-compatible.
The payoff uses existing surfaces rather than a new page, badge system, reward tree, or fourth beat.

scout_handoff.selected_surface: Use the filed Source to Shore `FIELD ATLAS` note resolved by `resolveFieldAtlasState(save)` from the filed `Dune Catch` branch in `src/engine/source-to-shore-state.ts`.
scout_handoff.copy_candidate: Filed: high source -> forest release -> coastal catch.
implementation_contract: Wait until `ECO-20260428-critic-450` clears the dedicated Source to Shore station container before promoting or implementing `ECO-20260428-main-454`.
Change only the filed Source to Shore payoff line in `src/engine/source-to-shore-state.ts`, preferably `liveAtlasNote` for `resolveDuneCatchState('filed')`.

Keep the payoff to one visible filed-memory line; do not also rewrite the archive strip, route-board summary, expedition card summary, route note filed text, or revisit-memory notices unless the station container removes the atlas seam.
If the post-container station page no longer displays the atlas note in filed Source to Shore, use the same one-line copy as the filed station-card notice fallback instead of adding a second surface.
Update exact-copy expectations in `src/test/field-season-board.test.ts` and `src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts`; add no new save fields or route phases.
science_and_copy_checks: The line should name the already-authored chapter relationship only; avoid stronger claims such as water creating forest shelter, dunes creating habitat, or plants making groundwater.
Keep the line under the existing `64` character atlas-note budget in `src/test/field-season-board.test.ts`.
No new `docs/science-source-ledger.md` row is needed if the copy only reuses `high source`, `forest release`, and `coastal catch`; add a ledger note only if main introduces a new ecological claim.
proof_expectations: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch"`
`npm test -- --run src/test/content-quality.test.ts -t "content quality"` if any route-note or content-facing copy outside `source-to-shore-state.ts` changes.
`npm run build` after runtime copy changes.
out_of_scope: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No new station page, atlas mode, journal page, notebook prompt, ecosystem-note resolver behavior, badge, reward, or economy.

Do not change `src/engine/field-requests.ts`, Route v2 filed-note text, debug snapshot builders, save schema, or route completion rules for this payoff unless a post-container review proves the selected seam disappeared.

guardrails: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No new notebook page, atlas mode, station tab, badge system, or reward economy.
Keep copy compact, kid-friendly, and source-safe.
Prefer one memorable payoff over several tiny scattered lines.
Do not alter route completion state to support the payoff unless scout proves it is necessary.
post_packet_path: Feed any remaining payoff ideas into later content packets after full-arc signoff.
Do not reopen Source to Shore breadth until the three-beat chapter has been playtested end to end.

runtime_changes: `src/engine/source-to-shore-state.ts` now uses the filed atlas note `Filed: high source -> forest release -> coastal catch.` for completed Source to Shore.
`src/test/field-season-board.test.ts` and `src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts` exact-copy expectations were updated for the filed atlas payoff.
preserved_scope: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No station page, atlas mode, journal page, badge, reward economy, save schema, route state, or Route v2 filed-note change.
No new science-source ledger row, because the copy only reuses the already-authored high-source, forest-release, and coastal-catch relationship.
proof: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch"`
critic_focus: Confirm the filed atlas payoff is satisfying but does not imply a hidden fourth beat.
Confirm the high-source -> forest-release -> coastal-catch wording is science-safe and kid-readable.
Confirm the implementation stayed on the selected one-line filed-memory seam.

checks: The payoff remains on the filed Source to Shore FIELD ATLAS note seam.
The copy is 54 characters, under the 64-character atlas-note budget.
The wording restates the existing high-source -> forest-release -> coastal-catch relationship without adding a new ecological claim.
The filed route board still shows exactly three completed beats and no hidden fourth beat.
No station page, journal page, atlas mode, notebook prompt, reward, badge, route state, or save schema was added.
proof: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch"`
critic_review.handoff: Packet 173 is clear from lane-2. Final full-arc signoff remains parked until the remaining packet reviews also clear.
